steps.title: Connect prediction and test
steps.content: Biologists predicted that feather structures vary with habitat temperature, but this had not been tested in mountain environments. Rao then studied Himalayan songbirds across elevations, which correspond to different temperatures.

steps.title: Reject overstatements
steps.content: Choice A says the findings contradict predictions, but they support them. Choice C says feathers lack flat sections, which the passage does not say. Choice D claims feather length matters more than structure, an unsupported comparison.

steps.title: State the supported main idea
steps.content: Rao found that high-elevation species had different feather structures and longer feathers, supporting a link between temperature and feather structure. The answer is B.
